channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India Considers Russian Oil to Reduce Dependence on Gulf Supplies Amid Strait of Hormuz Blockade
item.description: India relies heavily on imported oil, with over half coming from the Middle East and passing through the Strait of Hormuz. The recent closure of this strategic waterway has created a potential crisis ...

item.title: Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Paralympics: Key Events and Australian Competitors
item.description: The Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Paralympic Games are set to begin on 6 March, marking the 50th anniversary of the event. The Games will run until 15 March across northern Italy, in Milan, Cortina d'Amp...

item.title: Marcos emphasizes restraint and adherence to international law during US trip
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) highlighted the importance of President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr.'s upcoming working visit to the United States, especially given the ongoing tensions in ...

item.title: First UK government-chartered repatriation flight arrives from Oman carrying stranded British nationals amid Middle East conflict
item.description: The first British government-chartered repatriation flight from the Middle East landed at Stansted Airport in the early hours of Friday, 6 March 2026, bringing home some of the citizens stranded due t...

item.title: Government Urges Banks and Stakeholders to Support Livestock Sector Growth
item.description: The Tanzanian Government, through the Ministry of Livestock and Fisheries, is urging banks and financial institutions to provide loans to livestock production groups across the country. Dr Bashiru All...

item.title: European Airlines See Short-Term Gains Amid Middle East Airspace Closures
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has caused significant disruptions in the region's major international air hubs, such as Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and Doha. This has unintentionally benefited European...

item.title: Impact of the Strait of Hormuz Closure on Global Oil Markets
item.description: Following attacks on Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, approximately one-fifth of the world's oil supply has been removed from the market, causing a sharp rise in prices. Brent crude is cu...

item.title: Bianca Censori Attends Court Hearing for Kanye West Lawsuit in Modest Attire
item.description: Bianca Censori, known for her bold fashion choices, made headlines for a notably restrained appearance at a Los Angeles courthouse while supporting her husband, Kanye West. Opting for a modest dress a...

item.title: IAF Su-30MKI crashes in Assam; two pilots lose lives during training mission
item.description: In a tragic incident on March 6, 2026, two Indian Air Force (IAF) pilots lost their lives after a Su-30MKI fighter jet crashed in Assam's Karbi Anglong district. The aircraft was on a routine training...

item.title: Life in Utqiagvik, Alaska: Challenges and Traditions at the Northern Edge of the U.S.
item.description: Utqiagvik, formerly known as Barrow, is the northernmost inhabited town in the United States, located at 71 degrees north in Alaska. With more than five thousand residents, the town faces extreme livi...

item.title: Ngorongoro Conservation Area: A Unique Natural Heritage and Wildlife Sanctuary
item.description: Ngorongoro Conservation Area in Tanzania is one of the most unique natural places in the world. The area, formed from a huge volcanic caldera millions of years ago, is known for its amazing landscapes...

item.title: South-East Development Commission’s 2026 Budget Prioritises Travel and Soft Projects Over Infrastructure
item.description: The South-East Development Commission (SEDC) don release their 2026 budget for five states in Nigeria’s South-East, but e dey raise plenty wahala. The total budget na ₦140 billion, but people dey vex ...

item.title: Nigeria Faces Reduced Electricity Supply Due to Gas Shortages in Thermal Power Plants
item.description: Electricity wahala don increase for Naija as power generation don fall under 4,000 megawatts, mainly because gas wey thermal power plants dey use don reduce. According to the Nigerian Independent Syst...

item.title: Desalination Plants in the Persian Gulf Face Risk Amid Escalating Regional Conflict
item.description: Countries in the Persian Gulf, including Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, and Qatar, heavily rely on desalination plants powered by fossil fuels to provide drinking water for millions o...

item.title: Enforcement Directorate raids several Reliance Power offices over alleged bank fraud
item.description: On Friday, the Enforcement Directorate (ED) carried out extensive searches at multiple locations in Mumbai and Hyderabad linked to Reliance Power, a company under the Anil Ambani Group. The raids invo...

item.title: Daily Horoscope: Venus Shifts from Pisces to Aries and Its Influence on Each Sign
item.description: On 6 March 2026, astrologer Jemima Cainer highlights significant planetary shifts influencing the zodiac signs. Venus transitions from Pisces into Aries, creating a dynamic shift from emotional recept...

item.title: US signals cautious economic engagement with India, avoiding China-style advantages
item.description: At the 2026 Raisina Dialogue in New Delhi, US Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau stated that Washington will not repeat the same economic approach it had with China twenty years ago. While e...

item.title: Western Cape Premier Alan Winde challenges ethics breach finding over New York trip
item.description: Western Cape Premier Alan Winde has come under scrutiny after the provincial legislature’s conduct committee found him in breach of its code of conduct for failing to declare a sponsored trip to New Y...

item.title: NSCDC Leadership Transition Delayed as Outgoing Commandant-General Allegedly Refuses Handover to Senior Deputy Over Ethnic Concerns
item.description: The outgoing Commandant-General of the Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC), Ahmed Audi, don officially retire on Friday after him reach the mandatory retirement age and finish him tenure ...

item.title: Flightradar24 tracks global air traffic during crises and major events
item.description: Flightradar24, a Swedish flight tracking platform originally created as a tool to support a flight price comparison website, has become a leading resource for monitoring global aviation events in real...
